stimulus.content: After hiking up a mountain on a warm day, a student notices that clouds are forming near the top. The student remembers learning that warm air rises and cools as it goes higher in the atmosphere.

stem: Which part of the water cycle explains why clouds form near the mountain top?

choices.text: Evaporation turns water into gas near the peak
Condensation occurs when rising air cools down
Precipitation falls from clouds to the ground
Collection gathers water in lakes and streams

answer_explanation: Condensation is the process where water vapor cools and turns back into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. As warm air rises up the mountain and cools, condensation occurs.
